expectations.text: Raises, UNPROMPTED, that the deep-input test makes stack-safety BEHAVIORAL -- a transformation, not merely a refactoring (do not credit an echo of the word 'overflow' from the prompt)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: "This test made stack safety behavioral. ... sum(1_000_000) asserts a deep input returns the right number *without throwing* -- that pins stack safety as behavior. So the stack-safe form is the *transformation* that gets you from red to green here, not a later refactor."

expectations.text: Recommends the stack-safe form: (if -> while) + (variable -> assignment), or an explicitly-named trampoline
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Recommends a while-loop accumulator with code: "while (n !== 0) { // (if -> while) #10 ; acc = acc + n; // (variable -> assignment) #13 ; n = n - 1; }"

expectations.text: Does NOT offer a plain (V8) tail-recursive solution as the FINAL answer
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Tail-recursion is flagged as "a trap in TS/JS" (V8 has no reliable TCO, still overflows); final answer is the while-loop accumulator.

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
